title: ED self-harm documentation prompts
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact NICE psychosocial-assessment section supports documenting onset or context, severity or impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, the person’s concerns or goals and clinician risk context without using a prediction score.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

red_flags.text: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.
red_flags.rationale: The exact ED section supports measured vital data, focused physical and mental assessment, injury severity, appearance, immediate safety and safeguarding only when assessed; it does not predict future suicide.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.12–1.7.20 — emergency-department initial assessment and care

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact RCEM triage section independently supports documenting current distress, immediate safety, leaving risk, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status; no future-risk category is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental Health Triage and Initial Assessment

referral.text: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.
referral.rationale: The exact safeguarding and discharge section supports recording clinician-decided disposition, clinician-entered plan, completed risk discussion, specialist input and arranged handover only when completed.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE law and pathways govern local use

source_limitations: Method, intent, circumstances, injury, current thoughts, access to means, protective factors, capacity, intoxication, psychosocial formulation, legal status, treatment, observation and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
